content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a physical reflection of light and an internal cognitive act of rethinking, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to separate two core functions: a 'reflective projection' of the LLM's existing outputs that makes implicit reasoning visible, and a 'critical re-examination' step that actively corrects errors from the projected output. This dual-functional framing can resolve current limitations of single-perspective self-reflection that fails to catch consistent logical blind spots.
